titleStmt.title: Letter from David Wilson to WB Yeats, 4 April 1917
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: David Wilson

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

msIdentifier.repository: Harry Ransom Center, The University of Texas at Austin
msIdentifier.collection: William Butler Yeats Collection; II. Correspondence, 1891-1938, Outgoing. Box 8. Folder: Unidentified - Z (8.3)

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
66 Kenilworth Rd
St.Leonards-on-Sea 4.4.17 Dear Mr Yeats : Enclosed I send an inscription
which contains something like the Key numbers 11. As the
whole of it may be a language Dr Ross perhaps would
like to see it. The original was written on silver and
copied on to glass. I am anxious to know if this
is a language & if so what - for I used a special
method of trying to create an artificial rapport
I have not had much success with the photos lately.
I should also like to hear if those other inscriptions
I sent Dr Ross made any sense? I am sending this to you as you wrote about the
number 11. Sincerely yours
David Wilson
